metal_ratio_original: Fe:Mo = 2:1 by mass; total metal loading 30 wt%
precursor_summary: Fe(NO3)3·9H2O; (NH4)6Mo7O24; MgO fine particles; 25 mL DI water per precursor solution shown in Fig. 1
preparation_method: incipient/wet impregnation by slowly adding Mo solution into Fe solution, then depositing mixed solution on MgO
drying_condition: stir/dry at 90 °C to paste; oven dry at 90 °C for 4 h
calcination_condition: 500 °C; duration conflict: 3 h in text vs 5 h in Fig. 1
activation_summary: calcined oxide-form catalyst used directly; CH4-derived H2 may reduce/carburize catalyst during growth
crushing_or_sieving_condition: crushed after oven drying; no sieve size reported
phase_or_state_summary: calcined oxide precursor; Fe3C and Mo2C reported in products from non-reduced processes
dispersion_summary: not quantified; broad CNT diameter distribution suggests broad active-particle sizes
deactivation_reason: CNT product obstructing active surface; inferred by authors from conversion decline
evidence_text: 30 wt% FeMo/MgO with Fe:Mo=2:1 mass was prepared by impregnation, dried at 90 °C and calcined at 500 °C; non-reduced routes form carbide phases during growth.
evidence_location: PDF pp. 2-3, Catalyst preparation and Fig. 1; PDF pp. 7-9, XRD discussion
notes: Calcination duration requires human review because text and Fig. 1 conflict.
